description_long: This house features a traditional Japanese architectural style with a prominent gabled roof covered in dark tiles and wide eaves. The exterior walls are clad in horizontal wooden siding, complemented by white sliding shoji-style windows and doors. The structure is composed of a central main building with two symmetrical side wings, creating a balanced and harmonious appearance. The front entryway is defined by a small set of stone steps leading to a covered porch, with large windows providing natural light to the interior. The rear of the house mirrors the front's gabled design, while the sides display additional window groupings and a continuous roofline. The house sits on a flat, rectangular plot, suggesting a spacious and open setting.
description: A traditional Japanese-style house with a dark gabled roof, wooden siding, and shoji-style windows, featuring a central entryway and symmetrical wings.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entryway with stone steps, covered porch, and symmetrical wings with shoji-style windows.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the long horizontal layout, continuous roofline, and multiple window groupings along the facade.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the large gabled roof and symmetrical design, with minimal window openings and wooden paneling.
description_view_3: Opposite side view similar to view 1, showing the extended roof and additional window arrangements.
attribute_entryway_design: Central entry with stone steps, covered porch, and sliding shoji doors
